overview: Converts characters in text units between full-width (zenkaku) and half-width forms. For historical reasons, some Asian character sets have two display forms for certain characters; this step normalizes them in one direction or the other. The conversion is applied to the **target locale** text of each text unit — if no target text exists, the source is copied to the target before processing.

toHalfWidth.help:
Controls the conversion direction.

- **true** (default): Converts full-width characters to their half-width or ASCII equivalents. For example, `Ｑ` (U+FF31) → `Q` (U+0051) and `サ` (U+30B5) → `ｻ` (U+FF7B).
- **false**: Converts half-width and ASCII characters to their full-width equivalents. For example, `Q` (U+0051) → `Ｑ` (U+FF31) and `ｻ` (U+FF7B) → `サ` (U+30B5).

The additional options (`includeSLA`, `includeLLS`, `includeKatakana`, `asciiOnly`, `katakanaOnly`) refine what is included in the conversion.
notes: The conversion is applied to the **target locale** text. If no target text exists, the source text is first copied to the target and then processed.

asciiOnly.help:
When converting **to full-width** (`toHalfWidth` = false), restricts conversion to only ASCII characters. Half-width Katakana and other half-width characters are left unchanged.

Useful for normalizing Japanese text where half-width Katakana should stay half-width but ASCII alphanumerics should become full-width.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is false (converting to full-width)

katakanaOnly.help:
When converting **to full-width** (`toHalfWidth` = false), restricts conversion to only Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (`｡､｢｣`, etc.).

Useful for normalizing modern Japanese text where half-width Katakana should become full-width while alphanumeric characters remain half-width.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is false (converting to full-width)

includeSLA.help:
Also converts the **Squared Latin Abbreviations** from the CJK Compatibility block into sequences of non-CJK characters.

For example: `㏀` (U+33C0) → `kΩ` (U+006B, U+03A9).

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is true (converting to half-width)

includeLLS.help:
Also converts several characters from the **Letter-Like Symbols** block to character sequences:

| Symbol | Result |
|--------|--------|
| U+2100 | `a/c` |
| U+2101 | `a/s` |
| U+2105 | `c/o` |
| U+2103 | `°C` |
| U+2109 | `°F` |
| U+2116 | `No` |
| U+212A | `K` |
| U+212B | `Å` |

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is true (converting to half-width)

includeKatakana.help:
Also converts Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (`。、「」`, etc.) to their half-width forms.

Off by default to facilitate normalizing modern Japanese text: full-width alphanumeric characters can be converted to half-width while Katakana remains full-width.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when toHalfWidth is true (converting to half-width)

normalizeOutput.help:
Applies **Unicode NFC normalization** to the output text after conversion.

Converting half-width forms to full-width can produce decomposed sequences. For example, `ﾌﾟ` (U+FF8C U+FF9F) converts to `プ` (U+30D5 U+309A, decomposed), which NFC normalizes to `プ` (U+30D7, precomposed).

Enabled by default to ensure standard character representations are used.
notes: Normalization is only applied when at least one conversion has been made in the text unit.

processingNotes: The conversion operates on the **target locale** text of each text unit. If no target text exists, the source text is copied to the target before conversion.
The `asciiOnly` and `katakanaOnly` options apply only in the to-full-width direction; the `includeSLA`, `includeLLS`, and `includeKatakana` options apply only in the to-half-width direction.

examples.title: Normalize Japanese full-width alphanumerics to half-width
examples.description: Converts full-width ASCII characters to half-width while leaving Katakana in full-width form — a common normalization for modern Japanese text.
examples.config:
```yaml
toHalfWidth: true
includeKatakana: false
normalizeOutput: true
```

examples.title: Normalize half-width Katakana to full-width
examples.description: Converts only half-width Katakana to full-width while keeping ASCII characters in their original half-width form.
examples.config:
```yaml
toHalfWidth: false
katakanaOnly: true
normalizeOutput: true
```
